title: Registry Identity, SBOM, Provenance, Attestations, and Promotion · Pipeline Repair Bay
summary: A release responder inherits a plausible but defective attested image release record; preserve run evidence, isolate the first causal fault in registry identity, sbom, provenance, attestations, and promotion, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in attested image release record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Generate human-readable tags while binding promotion, deployment, rollback, and evidence to one registry digest.
Generate image SBOM and max-appropriate provenance, identify builder, source, inputs, subject digest, limits, and registry attachment.
Use actions/attest with least permissions and exact subject path or digest while recognizing plan, repository visibility, and registry constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary
steps.instruction:
Order the Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary delivery-system evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary.

Competency probe: Map actual build and source controls to SLSA 1.2 tracks and levels without claiming transitive dependency or code-quality guarantees.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-slsa-level-claim-boundary-1sxjxk". Tailor its attested-release.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary, keep Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-slsa-level-claim-boundary-1sxjxk
  - competency: ci-slsa-level-claim-boundary
    artifact: attested-release.yaml
    decision: slsa level claim boundary
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: event ref SHA workflow ref workflow SHA run ID and attempt
    case-key: 1sxjxk
    change: |
      subject: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified
      tags: [v2.4.0, stable]
      sbom: spdx-attached
      provenance: mode-max
      builder: github-hosted-reviewed-workflow
      actions-attest: 4
      attestation-subject: exact-digest
      verification: issuer-repository-workflow-builder-subject
      promotion: same-digest
      rollback: prior-verified-digest
    verify: verify image digest SBOM provenance signature issuer repository workflow builder subject predicate policy and promotion identity
    failure: Reject tags as identity, detached unbound SBOM, build-arg secrets, signature-only trust, unverified attestations, or transitive SLSA claims.
    repair: Bind metadata to digest, generate safe attestations, verify identity and expectations downstream, promote once, and state SLSA limits.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
